symptoms.symptom_name: Altered level of consciousness — unresponsive since impact

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Multi-system major trauma with haemorrhagic shock
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced consciousness after high-energy impact reflects severe TBI and/or cerebral hypoperfusion from shock.
Consistent with severe head injury, but does not by itself explain hypotension.

symptoms.symptom_name: Airway noise — snoring progressing to shallow irregular gasping breaths with blood in the airway

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Airway obstruction in the obtunded trauma patient
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Snoring/gurgling in a patient with reduced GCS signals partial airway obstruction — an immediate threat to life.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe external haemorrhage from scalp laceration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Scalp wounds bleed briskly; visible external blood loss is a controllable contributor to shock — direct pressure is the layperson intervention.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Femoral shaft fracture with associated blood loss
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A closed femoral shaft fracture can lose 1–1.5 L of blood into the thigh, contributing to shock.
Peripheral vasoconstriction and anaemia from acute blood loss produce pallor — a bystander-observable proxy for shock when a BP is unobtainable.
Unrestrained high-speed impact with cabin intrusion predicts occult internal injury and mandates trauma-team activation on mechanism alone.
Sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers circulatory compromise from pallor and mechanism, not a measured BP.
Isolated severe head injury classically causes hypertension (Cushing response); hypotension points to systemic haemorrhage.
Compensatory tachycardia to maintain cardiac output during acute blood loss — clinician-measured, sealed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe traumatic brain injury / cerebral hypoperfusion
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Formal GCS is a clinician assessment; GCS <=8 indicates a patient who cannot protect their airway.

symptoms.symptom_name: Asymmetrical paradoxical chest wall movement (flail chest)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Paradoxical segment movement on inspection/palpation — a clinician exam finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Gross pelvic instability on pelvic examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Unstable pelvic ring fracture with concealed haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pelvic instability signals a major source of concealed retroperitoneal bleeding — clinician exam, sealed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Respiratory compromise from flail chest / pulmonary contusion and shock
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured on a first-responder monitor; the bystander cannot obtain these values.

symptoms.symptom_name: Whole-body trauma CT and FAST ultrasound showing solid-organ injury and internal bleeding

investigation_details.expected_result: Free intraperitoneal fluid, solid-organ injury, thoracic and pelvic haemorrhage
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed internal haemorrhage; hospital investigation only, never available in telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Definitive imaging is a Level 1 trauma-centre determination; listed to mark the hospital pathway, not a telehealth expectation.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Multi-system major trauma with haemorrhagic shock
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: High-energy mechanism + altered consciousness + shock signs (pallor) + multi-system injury together are diagnostic of major trauma and mandate immediate trauma-team activation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: None obtainable via telehealth — escalate for pre-hospital and trauma-centre assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The remote service cannot perform any investigation; the correct 'investigation' is rapid transport to a Level 1 trauma centre for FAST and whole-body CT.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bystander self-assessment or attempts to check pulse/BP with equipment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Do not delay the 000 call or haemorrhage control to hunt for vital signs — time to definitive care is the priority.
